method_atom: empirical characterization of a large spatially-embedded interaction network, quantifying its heavy-tailed (power-law) degree distribution and a connection-probability that decays with pairwise spatial separation according to a gravity law (probability of a link falling off as an inverse power of distance), together with the spatial extent of closed triads which can span long ranges; complemented by a generative model in which mobile units migrate and locally adapt their connections, solved analytically and validated against stochastic simulation, reproducing the observed distance-dependent linking and long-range triads
object_atom: a mobile-phone communication network of 2.5 million customers linked by 810 million calls and text messages over six months, each customer tagged with a geographical home location
brief_reason: the transferable part is the spatial-network machinery (power-law degrees, gravity-law distance-decay of links, long-range triangles, and a migration/local-adaptation generative model); what is domain-specific is that the nodes are telecom customers and the links are phone calls and text messages placed at known home locations.
